evals.name: initialize-brownfield-dotnet
evals.prompt: Set up jaiba on this repo and build the brain. It's an existing .NET service we've been running for a while.

context.memory_state: empty (.ai/memory/ has no *.md, or bare templates)
code_facts: calls an external Payments API via a typed HttpClient; OpenAPI vendored at docs/openapi/payments-v2.yaml
Dockerfile + .github/workflows/ci.yml run `dotnet test` and a `snyk test` step
context.readme_state: non-empty but only a title and a one-line description
context.scriptfile: none; CI yml is the only place commands live

expected_output: Did the evidence sweep (code, .csproj, README, CI) before writing anything
Created/filled constitution.md, adr-log.md, reference-index.md from assets templates
constitution Quality Gate commands sourced from CI (dotnet test, snyk); did NOT invent commands
Asked the human for business objective and downstream consumers; marked [MISSING] for any deferred
adr-log kept only ADR-001 (dated today); did NOT back-fill pre-adoption architecture decisions
reference-index Payments API points at the vendored OpenAPI path, NOT the internal contracts assembly
snyk recorded under the secondary Workflow & Verification Tooling tier
README judged incomplete (missing requirements + how-to) → advised what to add, did NOT overwrite it
Wrote the brain in English; ended with an explicit list of remaining [MISSING]/[bracket] gaps per file

evals.name: initialize-empty-readme-fill
evals.prompt: Build the brain. Also our README.md is basically blank.
expected_output: README.md completely empty → filled from assets/readme-skeleton.md
Skeleton brackets filled from real evidence (commands from the justfile, stack from pyproject)
README allowed to be in the project/developer language (spanish), NOT forced to english
.ai/memory artifacts written in English regardless of session language
Quality Gate commands taken from the justfile, not invented
Closed with a gap report

evals.name: update-apply-proposed-adr
evals.prompt: Apply the ADR that the spec archive proposed for the itinerary permissions.
expected_output: Located the proposal in the spec archive
Showed the change in prose and asked for confirmation BEFORE writing (propose -> confirm -> enact)
Appended ADR-004 from the adr-log template, status flipped Proposed -> Accepted, dated today
Added ADR-004 to the Decision Index table
Did NOT edit or delete any prior ADR
Reported provenance (the spec archive it came from)

evals.name: update-fix-drift-new-integration
evals.prompt: The reference index doesn't mention the new payments integration we added last sprint — reconcile it.

context.memory_state: populated, reference-index has no payments entry
code_facts: no vendored Stripe spec, docs only at stripe.com/docs

expected_output: Scoped the drift to reference-index.md specifically (not a full brain re-derivation)
Added Stripe under External APIs (code-scope tier)
Consumption point grounded in real evidence (docs URL + STRIPE_API_KEY in .env.example), not invented
Did not read or echo the actual secret; pointed at .env.example
Proposed -> confirm -> enact; minimal change, untouched sections left alone
If any consumption detail was unfindable, marked [MISSING] rather than guessing

evals.name: update-rejects-non-identity-change
evals.prompt: We just bumped Serilog from 3.1 to 3.2 and refactored the order validator. Update the constitution.

context.change_nature: version bump + behavior-preserving refactor, no scope/consumer/gate/upstream change

expected_output: Recognized this is NOT a constitution event (no identity/scope/consumer/Quality Gate change)
Declined to edit constitution.md and explained why
Did not propose an ADR (no structural decision)
Pointed the developer at where such tactical changes are recorded instead (walkthrough/git), not memory

evals.name: mode-disambiguation-half-filled

context.memory_state: ambiguous: constitution.md has real content but reference-index.md is still a bare template

expected_output: Did not silently pick a mode
Surfaced the mixed state (some real content, some bare template) and asked whether to initialize the missing parts or update existing ones
Did not overwrite the populated constitution

evals.name: initialize-prunes-inapplicable-sections
evals.prompt: Build the brain for this repo.

code_facts: small standalone CLI tool, Go, single module
no external APIs, no datastore, no infra dependencies
no product/business documentation anywhere in the repo
no CI scanners or verification tooling beyond `go test`

expected_output: reference-index.md kept only the sections that apply (packages, if any)
Removed the External APIs, Infrastructure, Business Documentation and Workflow Tooling sections entirely — did NOT leave them as headings full of bracket rows
Remaining sections renumbered contiguously
Did NOT use [MISSING] for these — they are inapplicable categories, not unfound facts
Distinguished this from genuinely-unknown facts (e.g. business objective), which were asked or marked [MISSING]
